message:
Replace regex-based glob matching with O(n) char-level algorithm and remove dead charset code

The glob_match function in the policy module previously compiled a regex
from each glob pattern, cached it in a thread-local LRU, and matched
against it. This was unnecessarily complex and regex-heavy for simple
wildcard matching.

- Rewrite match_rule_glob in events.rs to use a char-level (not byte-level)
  O(n) algorithm with backtracking on '*', correctly handling multi-byte
  Unicode characters instead of operating on raw bytes
- Replace the entire regex-based glob_match in policy.rs with a one-liner
  delegation to events::match_rule_glob, eliminating the thread-local
  regex cache and the LRU dependency for this code path
- Remove dead code in ars_generalize.rs: ParsedCharset struct,
  parse_simple_charset(), validate_against_charset(), and their associated
  tests — these were unreferenced after prior pattern engine changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
